name: format
summary: Selects the output format (binary, MZ, PE, COFF, ELF, ELF64, ...).

name: use16
summary: Switch to 16-bit code generation.

name: use32
summary: Switch to 32-bit code generation.

name: use64
summary: Switch to 64-bit code generation.

name: use
summary: Activates a CPU/feature setting, e.g. "use bits64" or "use avx2" (multiple settings can follow, comma-separated). Provided by the alternate/advanced "x86-2" package, not the standard x86 package (which instead uses "use16"/"use32"/"use64").

name: org
summary: Set the address counter for the following code/data.

name: restartout
summary: Abandons all output generated so far and starts a fresh output area, optionally at a given base address (defaults to the current address). Like "org"/"section", cannot be used inside a "virtual" block.

name: entry
summary: Declare the program entry point. Not core language — provided per-format by the MZ/PE/ELF/Mach-O format packages, so available in practice wherever a format is selected.

name: section
summary: Core meaning: like "org", but also trims any preceding reserved (uninitialized) data. Overridden by the ELF/COFF/PE format packages to instead begin a named section with attributes, e.g. "section '.text' readable executable".

name: segment
summary: Begin a segment: a named MZ/PE segment in fasm1, or an ELF program-header segment with attributes like "readable"/"writeable"/"executable" in fasm2's ELF format package.

name: label
summary: Define a label, optionally with a size: "label name [:size]". The "at expr" clause is optional and overrides the default address (the current one) with an arbitrary expression, e.g. "label name [:size] at expr".

name: at
summary: Introduces the base address expression after "label ... at" or "virtual at".

name: virtual
summary: Begin a block whose contents are assembled without being written to output. Close with "end virtual". The base address defaults to the current one; "virtual at expr" overrides it. "virtual" can also take an existing area label as its sole argument to reopen a previously closed virtual block and append more data to it.

name: end virtual
summary: Closes a "virtual" block.

name: macro
summary: Define a macro. Close with "end macro" (fasm2) or a "{ }" body (fasm1).

name: end macro
summary: Closes a "macro" block (fasm2).

name: struc
summary: Defines a labeled macroinstruction (invoked as "label struc-name args" instead of a plain instruction), used to define structures. Close with "end struc" (fasm2) or a "{ }" body like "macro" (fasm1).

name: end struc
summary: Closes a "struc" block (fasm2).

name: struct
summary: Define a structure layout. Close with "ends". In fasm2/fasmg this isn't a core keyword itself — it's a friendlier macro built on "struc"/"namespace", provided by the bundled utility/struct.inc package (fasm1 conventionally provides an equivalent macro of its own).

name: ends
summary: Closes a "struct" block (see "struct").

name: if
summary: Conditional assembly. Close with "end if".

name: else if
summary: Ends the previously opened conditional block and opens a new one, assembled only when every earlier condition in the chain was false and this one is true. Any number of "else if"s may appear before at most one "else".

name: else
summary: Ends the previous conditionally-assembled block and begins one assembled only when none of the preceding "if"/"else if" conditions were true.

name: end if
summary: Closes an "if" block.

name: while
summary: Loop while a condition holds. Close with "end while".

name: end while
summary: Closes a "while" block.

name: repeat
summary: Repeat a block a fixed number of times. Close with "end repeat".

name: rept
summary: Synonym for "repeat": repeats a block a fixed number of times.

name: end repeat
summary: Closes a "repeat" block.

name: irp
summary: Synonym for "iterate": repeats a block once per comma-separated value, binding each to a name.

name: irpv
summary: Iterates a block once per value held by a symbolic variable, converting it into a regular parameter for the body.

name: iterate
summary: Iterate a block over a list of value tuples (synonym "irp").

name: end iterate
summary: Closes an "iterate" block.

name: break
summary: Stops a "repeat"/"while"/"iterate" block early, skipping its remaining repetitions.

name: indx
summary: Inside an iterated block, jumps the view of the iterated values to the repetition whose number is given by the argument, without physically repeating up to it — typically paired with "break" to inspect one specific iteration's values directly.

name: namespace
summary: Open a named scope for symbols. Close with "end namespace".

name: end namespace
summary: Closes a "namespace" block.

name: include
summary: Textually include another source file.

name: eval
summary: Assembles a sequence of bytes/strings given as its arguments as if it were source text — e.g. building an instruction's mnemonic dynamically at assembly time.

name: assert
summary: Fail assembly if the given condition is false.

name: esc
summary: Escapes its argument out of the current macro's own "macro"/"end macro" nesting count, so a macro can generate an unpaired "macro" or "end macro" line without unbalancing the definition containing it. Placed inside a nested definition, it isn't processed until the innermost macro is itself defined.

name: display
summary: Print text to the assembler's console output.

name: err
summary: Force an assembly error at this point.

name: retaincomments
summary: Switches the assembler to treat ";" as a regular token instead of stripping comments — needed to use semicolons literally, e.g. inside a "match" pattern. Revert with "removecomments".

name: removecomments
summary: Reverts "retaincomments", restoring the default behavior where ";" starts a comment.

name: isolatelines
summary: Prevents the assembler from combining source lines whose line break is preceded by "\". Revert with "combinelines".

name: combinelines
summary: Reverts "isolatelines", restoring the default combining of lines via a trailing "\".

name: local
summary: Declare macro-local symbol names (unique per expansion). Inside a "calminstruction", it instead forces the named symbol to be treated as local by giving it a defined-but-unusable placeholder value at the point "local" is encountered during the CALM instruction's *definition* — it emits no command into the compiled instruction.

name: outscope
summary: Available while any macroinstruction is processed: modifies the command that follows so that, if that command defines parameters, they're created in the recognition context of the caller of the macro currently running, rather than the macro body's own context — used to build context-sensitive helper macros (e.g. ones that declare a "local" name meant to be visible to the caller).

name: common
summary: Marks a macro block executed once regardless of repeat count.

name: forward
summary: Marks a macro block iterated forward over repeats.

name: reverse
summary: Marks a macro block iterated in reverse over repeats.

name: equ
summary: Defines a textual constant (macro-style substitution). Evaluates any symbolic-variable identifiers present in the assigned text at definition time (unlike "define"). Allows redefinition, preserving the previous value so "restore" can bring it back — to overwrite instead, use "reequ".

name: reequ
summary: Like "equ", but overwrites the symbol's value instead of stacking it — the symbolic-value counterpart to how "=" overwrites a regular value. The prior value is not recoverable with "restore".

name: define
summary: Defines a settable/redefinable symbolic constant. Unlike "equ", it does not evaluate symbolic-variable identifiers present in the assigned text — the text is stored as-is. Preserves the previous value (recoverable with "restore"); to overwrite instead, use "redefine".

name: redefine
summary: Redefines a symbol previously introduced with "define", discarding its earlier value instead of preserving it — the "define" counterpart to how "reequ" relates to "equ".

name: restore
summary: Discards the most recent redefinition of one or more symbols, bringing back their previous value — undoes a redefinition made with "equ"/"define" (which both preserve history) or with "=:", but not one made with "reequ"/"redefine"/"=" (which overwrite it).

name: purge
summary: Discards the definition of a macro (or other instruction-class symbol) — the same as "restore", but for instructions rather than values. A macro can remove its own definition this way.

name: restruc
summary: Like "purge", but for labeled instructions (ones defined with "struc") instead of plain macros.

name: mvmacro
summary: Moves a macro's definition from one name to another (effectively renaming it, or temporarily disabling it to restore later); the source name is left as if "purge"d.

name: mvstruc
summary: Like "mvmacro", but for labeled instructions (ones defined with "struc").

name: align
summary: Pad with zeroes/NOPs until the address counter is aligned to N. Not core language in fasm2 — provided as a calminstruction by the format/utility packages (core in fasm1).

name: times
summary: Repeat the following instruction/data N times. Not part of the bare fasmg engine or its standard x86 package — commonly provided by a bundled compatibility include for porting fasm1 sources (core in fasm1).

name: db
summary: Declare byte(s) (1 byte) of data.

name: dup
summary: Generates the same value multiple times inside a "db"/"dw"/... argument list: preceded by a numeric repeat count, followed by the value to repeat, e.g. "db 4 dup 90h". To duplicate a whole sequence of values, wrap them in parentheses: "db 2 dup ('abc',10)".

name: dw
summary: Declare word(s) (2 bytes) of data.

name: dd
summary: Declare doubleword(s) (4 bytes) of data.

name: dp
summary: Declare 6-byte data units, e.g. a far pointer (16-bit selector + 32-bit offset).

name: df
summary: Declare 6-byte data units (fasm1 synonym for "dp").

name: dq
summary: Declare quadword(s) (8 bytes) of data.

name: dt
summary: Declare ten-byte (80-bit) extended-precision data.

name: ddq
summary: Declare 16-byte data units (fasm2/fasmg only; matches the "dqword"/"xword" size).

name: dqq
summary: Declare 32-byte data units (fasm2/fasmg only; matches the "qqword"/"yword" size).

name: ddqq
summary: Declare 64-byte data units (fasm2/fasmg only; matches the "dqqword"/"zword" size).

name: du
summary: Declare word(s) (2 bytes) of data from a string, zero-extending each byte (fasm1 only).

name: rb
summary: Reserve N uninitialized bytes.

name: rw
summary: Reserve N uninitialized words.

name: rd
summary: Reserve N uninitialized doublewords.

name: rp
summary: Reserve N uninitialized 6-byte units.

name: rf
summary: Reserve N uninitialized 6-byte units (fasm1 synonym for "rp").

name: rq
summary: Reserve N uninitialized quadwords.

name: rt
summary: Reserve N uninitialized 10-byte units.

name: rdq
summary: Reserve N uninitialized 16-byte units (fasm2/fasmg only).

name: rqq
summary: Reserve N uninitialized 32-byte units (fasm2/fasmg only).

name: rdqq
summary: Reserve N uninitialized 64-byte units (fasm2/fasmg only).

name: file
summary: Include the raw bytes of an external file as data. Optionally followed by ":offset" and ",length" to copy only part of the file, e.g. "file 'data.bin':10h,4".

name: load
summary: Two distinct meanings. As a directive (fasm2/fasmg): defines a variable by reading a string of already-generated bytes back out of an output area, e.g. "load b:byte from 101h" or, via an area label, "load a:byte from hex_digits:10" (can even read data generated later in the same area); a third form, "from :offset" (":" then a bare offset), reads by raw offset in the output file instead of an area address, e.g. "load a:byte from :%-1". As a CALM command (inside a "calminstruction" block): the same idea but with a different syntax, always three comma-separated arguments (target variable, raw byte offset, byte count) operating on raw offsets rather than addresses.

name: store
summary: Two distinct meanings, the write-side counterpart to "load" in both. As a directive (fasm2/fasmg): overwrites already-generated bytes in an output area, e.g. "store value:1 at 101h" — can't touch data that hasn't been generated yet, and marks the area as "variable" (no longer readable in advance by "load"); a third form, "at :offset", writes by raw offset in the output file instead of an area address. As a CALM command: same idea with the CALM three-argument raw-offset syntax (offset, byte count, value).

name: calminstruction
summary: Defines a new instruction using the CALM low-level code-emission language. Close with "end calminstruction".

name: end calminstruction
summary: Closes a "calminstruction" block.

name: postpone
summary: Defers assembly of a block until after all following source text is processed. Close with "end postpone". Multiple postponed blocks assemble in reverse declaration order.

name: end postpone
summary: Closes a "postpone" block.

name: match
summary: Assembles its block only when the text given as its second argument (everything after the first comma, to end of line — not necessarily a macro argument) matches the pattern given by its first argument. Analogous to "if": chain alternatives with "else match pattern, text" and a final plain "else". Close with "end match" (fasm2) or a "{ }" body like "macro" (fasm1, no "else match" chaining). Inside a "calminstruction", an optional third argument supplies a bracket pair for balanced wildcard matching, or selects a custom modifier-delimiter character for typed wildcards (e.g. "name"/"expression"/"number"/"quoted"/"equ"/"macro"/"struc"/"area").

name: end match
summary: Closes a "match" block (fasm2).

name: rawmatch
summary: Like "match", but operates on the raw text of its second argument: does not evaluate symbolic variables in it, and strips any recognition-context information the text carries. Synonym "rmatch". Close with "end rawmatch".

name: rmatch
summary: Synonym for "rawmatch".

name: assemble
summary: Passes the text held by a symbolic variable directly to the assembler, unpreprocessed.

name: arrange
summary: The inverse of "match" — builds up a text value from a pattern and stores it in a symbolic variable.

name: compute
summary: Evaluates an expression and assigns the numeric result to a variable.

name: check
summary: Analogous to "if" — evaluates a condition and sets the result flag tested by "jyes"/"jno".

name: emit
summary: Two distinct meanings. As a base-language data directive (synonym "dbx"): generates data defined by a variadic, comma-separated list of values, like "db". As a CALM command (inside a "calminstruction" block, no "dbx" synonym there): takes exactly two pre-compiled-expression arguments — a byte length and an optional value (uninitialized data if the value is omitted; a string value must fit within the given length).

name: element
summary: As a directive: defines a special symbol usable as the variable of a linear-polynomial term. As an operator ("polynomial element index", high precedence, polynomial then index): extracts the *variable* of that polynomial's term at the given index (index 0 gives numeric 1, the constant term's own "variable") — see the "scale"/"metadata" operators (documented in hover's value-operator table) for the term's coefficient/size instead, and "elementof" for the same operator with arguments reversed.

name: sizeof
summary: Unary operator giving the byte size of a label/struct (same value as "metadata 0"), e.g. "length = sizeof table" or "sizeof.STRUCTNAME".

name: jump
summary: Unconditionally jumps to a label within the CALM instruction being defined.

name: jyes
summary: Jumps to a label only if the previous result-flag-setting command ("match", "check", "take", "taketext", or "transform") succeeded.

name: jno
summary: Jumps to a label only if the previous result-flag-setting command ("match", "check", "take", "taketext", or "transform") did not succeed.

name: exit
summary: Immediately terminates processing of the CALM instruction being defined (takes no arguments).

name: publish
summary: Assigns a value to a symbol whose name is given by the text held in a variable — the way to define a symbol whose name was computed (e.g. with "arrange") or passed in as an argument, rather than written literally. First argument: the variable holding the symbol's name; second: the value to assign. The first argument may be followed by ":" to make the symbol constant, or preceded by ":" to stack the value on top of any previous one (recoverable later with "restore").

name: transform
summary: Replaces identifiers of symbolic variables/constants within a text with their current values — the same substitution the "equ" directive performs. Sets the result flag to whether any replacement actually happened. An optional second argument names a namespace to interpret the text's identifiers in.

name: stringify
summary: Converts a variable's text into a string value, in place (like the "\`" preprocessing operator, but producing a real string rather than a quoted-string token).

name: take
summary: Pops the topmost value off a symbol's value stack onto another symbol (destination may be omitted to just discard it, like "restore"). Sets the result flag to whether a value was actually available — using the same symbol as source and destination lets you peek without removing it.

name: taketext
summary: Like "take", but only succeeds when the popped value is symbolic (tokenized text) rather than numeric — a common way to check a value is safe to inspect with "match".

name: call
summary: Directly executes another CALM instruction by name, passing values (not preprocessed text) as its arguments, skipping standard preprocessing/dispatch — the target must already be a CALM instruction. (A CALM instruction can also reach another one indirectly via "assemble", using normal instruction dispatch instead.)

name: initsym
summary: Assigns text to a local symbolic variable at the time the CALM instruction is *defined*, not each time it's called (unlike a normal argument) — commonly used to let a custom CALM command reach into the caller's local scope.
